pre_execution_gate.instruction: Before writing ANY code, read all attached files, then produce a summary of your understanding in the chat. The summary MUST include: 1. Task scope (what you will benchmark and why) 2. Test matrix dimensions (modes × bands × runs) 3. Baseline import strategy (Task 4.3 NAT=3 data) 4. PA security constraint (why sparse attention is AO/Code Agent only) 5. Evidence artifact path 6. Quality gate criteria (delta-based comparisons) 7. Governance updates required 8. Pre-condition verification checklist Wait for Lead Architect approval before proceeding.

pre_conditions.check: Branch: `feature/p5-task4-3b-sparse-attention` created from merged main HEAD (after merge of `feature/p5-task4-3-nat-sweep`). Verify HEAD contains Task 4.3 evidence artifact and SDO governance updates.

Task 4.3 evidence artifact exists: `phase2_gates/evidence/p5_task4_3_nat_sweep_matrix.json` Parse it, extract NAT=3 entries for bands [4096, 8192, 12288, 16384, 20480]. These are the Test A baselines — DO NOT re-run them.

Sparse attention API available: verify that `ov_genai.SchedulerConfig()` exposes `use_sparse_attention` (bool attribute) and `sparse_attention_config` (with `.mode`, `.num_retained_start_tokens_in_cache`, `.num_retained_recent_tokens_in_cache` sub-fields). If any attribute is missing, ABORT with `API_NOT_AVAILABLE` — do NOT attempt workarounds.

Verify `ov_genai.SparseAttentionMode.TRISHAPE` and `ov_genai.SparseAttentionMode.XATTENTION` exist as enum values. If either is missing, ABORT that specific test (B or C) and proceed with the available mode only.
Python .venv active with openvino_genai, psutil, transformers.
Test suite baseline: run `python -m pytest --tb=short -q --no-header` and record pass count. No test failures expected. Task 4.3b adds NO unit tests (benchmark harness only).

scope.summary: A/B test dynamic sparse attention (TRISHAPE and XATTENTION modes) against the Task 4.3 dense baseline at AO/Code Agent context bands. Determine whether sparse attention improves TTFT at long context, whether it interacts with speculative decoding acceptance rate, and whether it shifts the AR=0.000 collapse boundary discovered at ≥16K in Task 4.3. The PA is EXCLUDED — sparse attention must NEVER be enabled on the Policy Agent (security constraint).
scope.rationale: Task 4.3 discovered that speculative decoding acceptance rate collapses to exactly 0.000 at ≥16K for ALL NAT values. This is the most consequential finding in the Task 4 series. Sparse attention changes KV cache contents by evicting intermediate tokens — this may shift the collapse boundary. Intel's technical article (2026-05-06) demonstrates 2.6× TTFT improvement at 32K on identical hardware (Core Ultra 7 258V / Arc 140V). If sparse attention significantly improves TTFT or shifts AR collapse, it becomes a mandatory AO/Code Agent configuration. Running this before Task 4.4 (XAttention sweep) provides data for potential sparse+XAttention cross-interaction testing.
scope.pa_security_constraint: NEVER enable sparse attention on the Policy Agent. The PA system prompt is ~600 tokens. With `num_retained_start_tokens_in_cache = 128`, only the first 128 tokens are guaranteed as attention sinks. Tokens 129–600 of the system prompt (the critical policy rules) may be evicted from the KV cache at long context. A policy rule that is evicted cannot influence the classification decision — this is an UNACCEPTABLE security regression. Sparse attention is AO + Code Agent ONLY. This constraint is not negotiable.
scope.harness_declaration: NO_FULL_HARNESS — new script, no prior sparse attention benchmark. Pattern derived from run_p5_task4_3_nat_sweep.py (list-input generate, RSS sampler, acceptance rate extraction, atomic JSON writes, AC power enforcement).
scope.no_production_code_changes: Task 4.3b touches phase2_gates/ and docs/ ONLY. No modifications to services/, shared/, launcher/, or tests/.

dimension.name: sparse_mode
dimension.note: DENSE (Test A): imported from Task 4.3 NAT=3 baseline — NOT re-run. TRISHAPE (Test B): tri-shape pattern (dense start sinks + dense recent + sparse middle). XATTENTION (Test C): sparse XAttention blocks only (no tri-shape wrapper). NAMING DISAMBIGUATION: SparseAttentionMode enum values are SCHEDULER-LEVEL KV cache eviction patterns — completely separate from GPU_ENABLE_SDPA_OPTIMIZATION (GPU plugin SDPA kernel, tested in P5-005b as OFF=better). These are NOT the same feature.

dimension.name: prompt_band
dimension.note: PA bands (512, 2048) EXCLUDED — sparse attention must never touch PA workloads. 4096: AO baseline reference band, cross-validates with Task 4.3. 8192: AO/Code Agent primary production band. 12288: regime change boundary (NAT=3 shows 38% TPS penalty in Task 4.3). 16384: AR=0.000 collapse boundary — key test for sparse attention impact. 20480: ceiling validation (OPTIONAL — graceful OOM handling).

calibration_check.instruction: Before running Tests B and C, compile a DENSE pipeline (identical to Task 4.3 construction — no sparse attention) and run 1 generate() call at band 4096 with NAT=3. Compare the TPS to the Task 4.3 baseline (8.065 tps). If within ±15%: proceed (thermal/session variance is acceptable). If outside ±15%: flag as CALIBRATION_WARNING in the evidence but proceed anyway. This is a defensive sanity check, NOT a quality gate blocker.

test_matrix.execution_order:
1. Load Task 4.3 baseline (Test A import from evidence JSON) 2. Run calibration check (1 dense generate at 4K) 3. Compile TRISHAPE pipeline 4. Run Test B: TRISHAPE at [4096, 8192, 12288, 16384, 20480] (band-ascending) 5. Delete TRISHAPE pipeline, gc.collect() 6. Compile XATTENTION pipeline 7. Run Test C: XATTENTION at [4096, 8192, 12288, 16384, 20480] (band-ascending) 8. Compute deltas and quality gates
Pipeline compilations: 3 total (1 dense calibration + 1 TRISHAPE + 1 XATTENTION). The dense calibration pipeline is discarded after the single check — it does NOT serve as the Test A data source (Task 4.3 evidence JSON is the Test A source).

pipeline_configs.config: scheduler = SchedulerConfig() scheduler.cache_size = 3 # No sparse attention — identical to Task 4.3 construction pipeline = LLMPipeline( str(target_path), "GPU", scheduler_config=scheduler, draft_model=ov_genai.draft_model(str(draft_path), "GPU"), )

scheduler = SchedulerConfig() scheduler.cache_size = 3 scheduler.use_sparse_attention = True scheduler.sparse_attention_config.mode = ov_genai.SparseAttentionMode.TRISHAPE # All other sparse_attention_config fields use defaults: # num_retained_start_tokens_in_cache = 128 (attention sinks) # num_retained_recent_tokens_in_cache = 1920 (working window) # num_last_dense_tokens_in_prefill = 100 # xattention_block_size = 64 # xattention_stride = 8 # xattention_threshold = 0.80 pipeline = LLMPipeline( str(target_path), "GPU", scheduler_config=scheduler, draft_model=ov_genai.draft_model(str(draft_path), "GPU"), )

scheduler = SchedulerConfig() scheduler.cache_size = 3 scheduler.use_sparse_attention = True scheduler.sparse_attention_config.mode = ov_genai.SparseAttentionMode.XATTENTION # Same defaults as TRISHAPE for xattention_* fields. # Difference: no tri-shape wrapper (no dense-start/dense-recent guarantee). pipeline = LLMPipeline( str(target_path), "GPU", scheduler_config=scheduler, draft_model=ov_genai.draft_model(str(draft_path), "GPU"), )

test_matrix.generation_config: # Fixed for ALL configs (NAT=3 LOCKED from Task 4.3): cfg = ov_genai.GenerationConfig() cfg.max_new_tokens = 128 cfg.do_sample = False cfg.temperature = 0.0 cfg.top_k = 1 cfg.top_p = 1.0 cfg.num_assistant_tokens = 3 # NAT=3 — LOCKED cfg.assistant_confidence_threshold = 0.0
test_matrix.prompt_construction: Use IDENTICAL prompt construction to Task 4.3: - Tokenizer: AutoTokenizer from Qwen3-14B model directory. - System prompt: "You are a helpful assistant." - User content: padded to target token count per band. - Chat template: apply_chat_template with add_generation_prompt=True. - Build prompt ONCE per band, reuse across sparse modes. Prompts MUST be byte-identical to Task 4.3 prompts at matching bands to ensure the ONLY variable is the sparse attention configuration.
test_matrix.band_20k_oom_handling: The 20,480 band is OPTIONAL. Apply the same defensive handling as Task 4.3: 1. Save intermediate results after EACH band (bands 4096-16384 preserved). 2. Wrap 20K in try/except (RuntimeError, MemoryError, Exception). 3. If OOM at 20K for a sparse mode: mark as "OOM_SKIPPED", continue. 4. If RSS exceeds 26,000 MB: abort 20K immediately. 5. Session is NOT a failure if 20K OOMs. Note: Task 4.3 completed 20K without OOM (peak RSS 1,835 MB). Sparse attention SHOULD reduce RSS at long context (evicts old KV entries). OOM at 20K with sparse enabled would be UNEXPECTED and must be prominently flagged.

per_run.field: Wall-clock tokens/second for full target+draft pipeline. Source: extended_perf_metrics.get_throughput().mean
Mean draft model forward pass time. Source: extended_perf_metrics.draft_model_metrics.get_inference_duration().mean
NAT × total_speculative_episodes. Source: raw_metrics.m_batch_sizes count × 3 (NAT=3 fixed)
sum(b - 1 for b in m_batch_sizes). Source: raw_metrics.m_batch_sizes
Per-step array [step_1_rate, step_2_rate, step_3_rate]. Source: m_batch_sizes analysis (3 steps since NAT=3)
Process RSS high-water mark. Source: RssSampler thread (0.01s polling interval)
First-token wall-clock time. PRIMARY METRIC for sparse attention. Source: extended_perf_metrics.get_ttft().mean

supplementary.field: Time to compile Test B pipeline.
Time to compile Test C pipeline.

mandatory_measurements.validation_rule: A run missing ANY of the 7 fields is INVALID and must not be counted. If valid_count drops below 3 for any config, mark as INSUFFICIENT_DATA.

api_critical_patterns.pattern: pipeline.generate([prompt], gen_config, streamer_callback) NEVER: pipeline.generate(prompt, gen_config, streamer_callback) Bare str input returns bare str with NO perf_metrics, NO extended_perf_metrics. List input returns DecodedResults with .texts[0], .perf_metrics, .extended_perf_metrics.

output = pipeline.generate([prompt], gc, cb) epm = output.extended_perf_metrics epm.get_throughput().mean # native combined TPS epm.get_ttft().mean # native TTFT (ms) — PRIMARY METRIC epm.draft_model_metrics.get_inference_duration().mean # draft forward ms epm.get_num_accepted_tokens() # integer accepted count

raw = output.perf_metrics.raw_metrics batch_sizes = list(raw.m_batch_sizes) # batch_sizes[i] = accepted_tokens + 1 for speculative episode i # Aggregate: sum(b-1 for b in batch_sizes) / (3 × len(batch_sizes))

baseline_import.instruction: Load `phase2_gates/evidence/p5_task4_3_nat_sweep_matrix.json` at script startup. Parse the `results` array and extract all entries where `nat == 3` for bands [4096, 8192, 12288, 16384, 20480]. These become the Test A (DENSE) baselines. For each baseline entry, extract: - combined_tps (summary.combined_tps.mean) - ttft_ms (summary.ttft_ms.mean) - acceptance_rate_aggregate (summary.acceptance_rate_aggregate) - peak_rss_mb (summary.peak_rss_mb — or from per-band peak) Store these in the evidence artifact under test_a_baseline.

reference_data.note: Task 4.3 execution report — NAT=3 per-band (informational only; use JSON)

baseline_import.ar_collapse_context: At bands 16384 and 20480, acceptance_rate is EXACTLY 0.000 for ALL NAT values in Task 4.3 — speculative decoding has completely failed. The Qwen3-0.6B draft model generates tokens that the Qwen3-14B target 100% rejects beyond ~12K context. SPARSE ATTENTION MAY SHIFT THIS BOUNDARY because it changes which KV cache entries are retained. If AR > 0 at 16K with sparse attention enabled, that is a significant finding.

quality_gate.gate: All 7 mandatory fields present for all measured runs at bands [4096..16384]. 20K band exempt (OOM acceptable). Threshold: zero invalid runs at bands 4096-16384.
valid_count >= 5 for all non-OOM configurations at bands [4096..16384]. If any config has valid_count < 5, disposition = INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE.

For each band ≥8192, compute: ttft_delta_pct = (ttft_dense - ttft_sparse) / ttft_dense × 100 If ttft_delta_pct ≥ 10% at ANY band ≥8192 for EITHER mode (TRISHAPE or XATTENTION): → Flag that (mode, band) as SPARSE_CANDIDATE If ttft_delta_pct ≥ 10% at TWO OR MORE bands ≥8192: → Flag mode as STRONG_SPARSE_CANDIDATE If ttft_delta_pct < 10% at ALL bands: → TTFT improvement is marginal; sparse attention benefit is primarily RSS, not latency.

For each band, compute: tps_ratio = tps_sparse / tps_dense If tps_ratio ≥ 0.95 (within 5% of baseline): SPARSE_COMPATIBLE — no throughput cost. If tps_ratio < 0.95 but ≥ 0.85: SPARSE_MINOR_COST — acceptable for TTFT trade. If tps_ratio < 0.85: SPARSE_TPS_DEGRADATION — significant throughput cost.

For each band, compute: ar_delta = ar_sparse - ar_dense If ar_delta < -0.10 (>10% AR degradation) at ANY band ≤12288 (where AR > 0 in baseline): → Flag as SPEC_DECODE_INTERACTION — sparse attention is harming draft acceptance. SPECIAL CASE for bands 16384+: if ar_sparse > 0.0 (vs ar_dense = 0.000): → Flag as AR_COLLAPSE_BOUNDARY_SHIFT — sparse attention has RECOVERED speculative decoding at long context. This is a MAJOR finding. Report prominently. If ar_sparse == 0.0 and ar_dense == 0.0 at 16K/20K: no interaction, as expected.

For each band, compare: rss_delta = rss_sparse - rss_dense Expected: rss_delta ≤ 0 (sparse should reduce RSS by evicting old KV entries). If rss_delta > 0 at ANY band: flag as UNEXPECTED_RSS_INCREASE. If rss_delta < 0 by >10% at ANY band ≥8192: flag as RSS_IMPROVEMENT. RSS improvement at 16K+ is particularly significant (Task 4.3 showed 3,562 MB dense).
peak_rss_mb at the highest successful band must be < 15,507 MB. If exceeded: MEMORY_BUDGET_EXCEEDED.

Compare TRISHAPE vs XATTENTION on all metrics at each band. Identify which mode is superior (or if they are equivalent within noise). Record the winning mode per band and overall. TRISHAPE is expected to be safer (preserves attention sinks); XATTENTION may be faster but risks degrading quality at position-sensitive tasks.
quality_gate.disposition_rules:
G-01/G-02 FAIL → INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE (cannot draw conclusions)
G-03 STRONG_SPARSE_CANDIDATE + G-04 COMPATIBLE + G-05 no SPEC_DECODE_INTERACTION: → disposition = SPARSE_ENABLED_AO_CODE → Update AO + Code Agent workload profiles to enable sparse attention. → Record winning mode (TRISHAPE or XATTENTION) in ADR-012 §2.2. → Confirm 4K band uses same mode (or if 4096 shows no benefit, sparse OFF at 4K).
G-03 marginal/FAIL OR G-05 SPEC_DECODE_INTERACTION OR G-04 TPS_DEGRADATION: → disposition = SPARSE_DEFERRED → Insufficient benefit or negative interaction detected. → Close, proceed to Task 4.4. Note findings in ADR-012 for future reference.
G-05 AR_COLLAPSE_BOUNDARY_SHIFT detected: → This overrides other gates as a MAJOR finding regardless of disposition. → Must be documented prominently in the execution report. → SDO will decide whether to add a follow-up task for detailed AR boundary mapping.
ANY run failure that prevents ≥3 valid runs at bands 4096-16384: → disposition = INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE

governance_updates.update: Update ADR-012 §2.2 — add new row to configuration table: | **Sparse attention** | [winning mode] AO/Code only; PA=OFF | **LOCKED** | Task 4.3b: [summary]. Evidence: p5_task4_3b_sparse_attention_ab_test.json | Add sparse_attention_config defaults to the row notes.
Update ADR-012 §2.2 — add new row: | **Sparse attention** | OFF (globally) | **LOCKED** | Task 4.3b: DEFERRED — [reason]. Evidence: p5_task4_3b_sparse_attention_ab_test.json |
Update ADR-012 §2.2 — add new row: | **Sparse attention** | OFF (default) | EVALUATING | Task 4.3b: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — [reason]. May require retry. |

Add prominent note to ADR-012 §3.1 (architectural implications): "Sparse attention shifts the speculative decoding AR collapse boundary beyond 16K. AR at 16K with [mode]: [value]. This may warrant re-evaluation of DEC-02 (graceful degradation acceptance) if AR recovery is substantial enough for the Code Agent workload profile at 16K."

Append new LEDGER entry to docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md. Use the NEXT available entry number (count existing ### Entry N headers + 1). Format:
### Entry [N] — Task 4.3b: Dynamic Sparse Attention A/B Test
**Date:** 2026-03-0X (actual execution date) **Branch:** `feature/p5-task4-3b-sparse-attention` **Commit:** `[HEAD after commit]` **Type:** Empirical Evidence Collection (Configuration Optimization) **Scope:** Sparse attention A/B test — TRISHAPE + XATTENTION vs dense baseline at bands [4096, 8192, 12288, 16384, 20480] with NAT=3 LOCKED.
Key findings: - [TTFT improvement summary per mode] - [TPS compatibility summary] - [AR interaction / collapse boundary shift status] - [RSS delta summary] - [Mode comparison: TRISHAPE vs XATTENTION]
Quality gate: [G-01..G-08 results] Disposition: [SPARSE_ENABLED_AO_CODE / SPARSE_DEFERRED / INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE]
Evidence: `phase2_gates/evidence/p5_task4_3b_sparse_attention_ab_test.json`

execution_prompt.intermediate_save_strategy: Save partial results after EACH band completes (both TRISHAPE and XATTENTION). Use `p5_task4_3b_sparse_attention_ab_test.json.partial` for intermediate saves. After all bands complete (or 20K OOM), rename to final artifact path. Save Test A baseline import as the first intermediate save (before any generate calls).
execution_prompt.commit_template:
feat(benchmark): Task 4.3b — sparse attention A/B test (AO/Code Agent)
A/B test dynamic sparse attention (TRISHAPE + XATTENTION) against dense baseline (imported from Task 4.3) at bands [4096,8192,12288,16384,20480] with NAT=3 LOCKED. AO/Code Agent workload only — PA excluded (security).
- 10 new configurations (2 modes × 5 bands), 5 measured runs each - Dense baseline imported from Task 4.3 evidence JSON (not re-run) - TTFT primary metric; TPS, AR, RSS secondary - Disposition: [SPARSE_ENABLED_AO_CODE | SPARSE_DEFERRED | INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE] - ADR-012 §2.2 updated: sparse attention row [LOCKED|EVALUATING] - LEDGER Entry [N] - AR collapse at 16K: [SHIFTED | UNCHANGED]
Evidence: phase2_gates/evidence/p5_task4_3b_sparse_attention_ab_test.json

baseline.name: Task 4.3 NAT=3 per-band (dense, from execution report)

reference_data.baseline: Band 4096: tps=8.065, ar=0.457, peak_rss=6,372 MB Band 8192: tps=5.543, ar=0.378, peak_rss=5,707 MB Band 12288: tps=2.482, ar=0.378, peak_rss=3,569 MB Band 16384: tps=2.886, ar=0.000, peak_rss=3,562 MB Band 20480: tps=3.207, ar=0.000, peak_rss=1,835 MB
Pipeline compile time: 26,802 ms Standalone draft TPS: 76.26 (mean 3 runs at 4K) NAT=3 global weighted TPS score: 4.847 (highest of all NATs)

Dense TTFT at 32K: ~88 seconds Sparse TTFT at 32K: ~34 seconds (2.6× improvement) Config: OpenVINO 2025.2.0-dev, Core Ultra 7 258V, Arc 140V Note: different OV version, no speculative decoding, different model. Directional only.
reference_data.note: Task 4.3 and Task 4.3b use different pipeline configurations (dense vs sparse). The imported baseline is cross-pipeline but same-hardware and same-model. Deltas are valid for architectural decisions. The calibration check (step 2) validates that session-to-session variance is within tolerance.

execution_prompt.runtime_estimate: Baseline import + calibration check: ~2 minutes (1 compile + 1 generate + JSON parse) TRISHAPE pipeline compilation: ~20-30 seconds XATTENTION pipeline compilation: ~20-30 seconds Generate calls per mode: 35 (5 bands × 7 runs per band) 4096 band: 7 calls × ~12 sec = ~1.5 min 8192 band: 7 calls × ~18 sec = ~2 min 12288 band: 7 calls × ~25 sec = ~3 min 16384 band: 7 calls × ~32 sec = ~4 min 20480 band: 7 calls × ~40 sec = ~5 min (if no OOM) Subtotal per mode: ~16 minutes Total for both modes: ~32 minutes Total with compilation + calibration + analysis: ~45-60 minutes Ceiling estimate (with OV recompile overhead): ~75 minutes Well within single-session ceiling.
execution_prompt.attachment_order: 1. .github/copilot-instructions.md (governance — MUST) 2. docs/Task4.3b_v1.xml (this prompt — PRIMARY) 3. docs/P5_TASK4_PRODUCTION_CONFIG_FEASIBILITY.md (spec reference — §Task 4.3b, §6 measurements) 4. docs/adrs/ADR-012-Qwen3-14B-Model-Selection-Speculative-Decoding.md (parameter table)

verification_commands.command: python -c "print([l.strip() for l in open('docs/adrs/ADR-012-Qwen3-14B-Model-Selection-Speculative-Decoding.md') if 'Sparse attention' in l])"
